template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS HIPAA AUTHORIZATION (STAND-ALONE RELEASE)**:
Stand-alone authorization for the release of protected health information (PHI), executed by the individual (or, for a deceased individual, the personal representative) pursuant to the federal Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy Rule, 45 C.F.R. Parts 160 and 164, and consistent with Massachusetts confidentiality law (M.G.L. c. 111, § 70 for hospitals; M.G.L. c. 112, § 12CC for physicians; M.G.L. c. 123, § 36 for mental health records).

Used in estate planning to supplement a Health Care Proxy, empower agents/trustees pre-incapacity, and give family members access to information needed to make timely decisions. Target length: 2 pages.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `scope`: `full_release` (default — all PHI) | `limited_by_category` (exclude specific categories).
- `excludeCategories`: array — optional sensitive categories to EXCLUDE from the release when `scope` = `limited_by_category`: `mental_health_records`, `substance_use_disorder_records`, `hiv_aids_records`, `genetic_testing_records`, `reproductive_health`.
- `expirationType`: `death_of_individual` (default) | `specific_date` | `upon_revocation` | `specific_event`.
- `specificDate`: string (ISO format) — required when `expirationType` = `specific_date`.
- `includeBroadAgentList`: boolean (default true) — authorize both named agents (healthcare agent, attorney-in-fact, trustee) and immediate family members.
- `posthumous`: boolean — when true, include language authorizing post-death disclosures under 45 C.F.R. § 164.502(f) to specified recipients.

**STRUCTURE**:

## AUTHORIZATION FOR THE USE AND DISCLOSURE OF PROTECTED HEALTH INFORMATION (HIPAA)

### I. IDENTIFICATION OF INDIVIDUAL
Name of Individual: [Individual full legal name]
Date of Birth: [DOB]
Address: [address]
Social Security Number: [LAST FOUR DIGITS ONLY]

### II. AUTHORIZED RECIPIENTS
I hereby authorize all health care providers, health plans, health care clearinghouses, business associates, insurers, pharmacies, laboratories, hospitals, medical practices, nursing homes, assisted living facilities, home health agencies, hospice programs, rehabilitation facilities, and mental health providers (collectively, 'Covered Entities') that have treated me or maintain any of my protected health information ('PHI') to USE and DISCLOSE my PHI to the following recipients:

1. **Primary Authorized Recipient**: [Name], [relationship], [address], [phone]
2. **Secondary Authorized Recipient**: [Name], [relationship], [address], [phone]
3. [If `includeBroadAgentList`]: My Health Care Agent and Successor Agents named in my Health Care Proxy dated [date]; my Agent under my Durable Power of Attorney dated [date]; the Trustee of my [Trust name] dated [date]; my attorney [Attorney name and firm]; my accountant [CPA name]; and the following family members: [list names and relationships].

The above-named recipients are authorized to receive, review, copy, and discuss my PHI with Covered Entities, without further authorization from me.

### III. INFORMATION COVERED BY THIS AUTHORIZATION
[Based on `scope`]:

- **full_release**: This authorization covers any and all of my PHI, including but not limited to: medical records, treatment notes, laboratory and diagnostic test results, imaging studies, medication histories, billing and insurance records, and any other information received, created, or maintained by a Covered Entity concerning my health, past, present, or future condition or treatment.

- **limited_by_category**: This authorization covers all of my PHI EXCEPT for the following categories, which require a separate, specific authorization: [list `excludeCategories`].

### IV. SPECIAL CATEGORIES OF INFORMATION (EXPRESS CONSENT)
Except as expressly excluded in § III above, I expressly consent to the disclosure of the following sensitive categories of PHI, consistent with the heightened protections under state and federal law:
(a) **Mental health records** — including psychotherapy notes (with the understanding that psychotherapy notes under 45 C.F.R. § 164.501 require separate authorization; by executing this document I consent to their disclosure); records under M.G.L. c. 123, § 36; Department of Mental Health records.
(b) **Substance use disorder records** — disclosure protected under 42 C.F.R. Part 2; I expressly consent to disclosure of such records covered by Part 2 consistent with 42 C.F.R. § 2.31.
(c) **HIV/AIDS-related information** — disclosure governed by M.G.L. c. 111, § 70F; I expressly consent.
(d) **Genetic information** — including information protected by the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act (GINA), 42 U.S.C. § 2000ff et seq.
(e) **Reproductive health / sexually transmitted infection records**.

### V. PURPOSE OF DISCLOSURE
The purposes of disclosure include, without limitation: coordination of my health care and treatment; assistance with medical decision-making; communication with family members and authorized agents; administration of my estate plan (Will, Revocable Trust, Durable Power of Attorney, Health Care Proxy); assistance with insurance claims and appeals; legal representation; and such other purposes as I may request or as the authorized recipient determines in good faith.

### VI. EXPIRATION
[Based on `expirationType`]:
- **death_of_individual**: This authorization shall remain in effect until my death, unless earlier revoked in writing, and the individual executing this Authorization expressly acknowledges the post-death provisions in § VIII below.
- **specific_date**: This authorization shall expire on [`specificDate`], unless earlier revoked.
- **upon_revocation**: This authorization shall remain in effect indefinitely until revoked in writing.
- **specific_event**: This authorization shall expire upon [describe event].

### VII. RIGHT TO REVOKE
I understand that I have the right to revoke this authorization in writing at any time, except to the extent that a Covered Entity has already taken action in reliance on this authorization. A written revocation must be delivered to the Covered Entity's Privacy Officer and to the Authorized Recipients named in § II. Revocation does not affect disclosures already made under this authorization. Revocation rights consistent with 45 C.F.R. § 164.508(b)(5).

### VIII. [IF posthumous] POST-DEATH DISCLOSURES
Consistent with 45 C.F.R. § 164.502(f) and § 164.502(g)(4), I authorize Covered Entities to disclose my PHI after my death to the following persons for purposes of administering my estate, fulfilling my funeral and disposition wishes, coordinating organ donation, and responding to family inquiries: the Personal Representative of my estate; my Health Care Agent and Successor Agents; the Trustee of my Revocable Trust; and the family members named in § II.3 above. Protected health information of a deceased individual ceases to be protected 50 years after the date of death, consistent with 45 C.F.R. § 164.502(f).

### IX. REDISCLOSURE NOTICE
I understand that once my PHI has been disclosed pursuant to this authorization, it may no longer be protected by HIPAA and may be subject to redisclosure by the recipient, although Massachusetts law may restrict further disclosure under M.G.L. c. 111, § 70E (patient's right to privacy) and c. 214, § 1B (right of privacy).

### X. VOLUNTARY NATURE AND CONDITIONS
I understand that:
- Signing this authorization is voluntary;
- A Covered Entity may NOT condition treatment, payment, enrollment, or eligibility for benefits on whether I sign this authorization, except as permitted under 45 C.F.R. § 164.508(b)(4);
- I may receive a copy of this authorization upon request.

### XI. GOVERNING LAW
This authorization is executed under and governed by the federal HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 C.F.R. Parts 160 and 164) and the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts.

### SIGNATURE

_______________________________
[Individual full legal name] (or Personal Representative, if applicable)
Date: ________________

[If signed by a personal representative (parent of minor, guardian, or personal representative of decedent), state the basis for authority:]
Authority basis: ____________________________________

### WITNESS (OPTIONAL)
_______________________________
Witness printed name, address, and date

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite federal authority exactly: `45 C.F.R. § 164.508` (core authorization requirements), `§ 164.502(g)` (personal representatives), `§ 164.502(f)` (decedent PHI), `§ 164.501` (psychotherapy notes definition). Cite `42 C.F.R. Part 2` for substance use disorder records.
- Cite Massachusetts law exactly: `M.G.L. c. 111, § 70` (hospital records), `§ 70E` (patient rights), `§ 70F` (HIV/AIDS records). Cite `M.G.L. c. 112, § 12CC` (physician records). Cite `M.G.L. c. 123, § 36` (mental health records).
- MUST include the core elements required by `45 C.F.R. § 164.508(c)`: description of information, identity of recipient, expiration, revocation rights, and a statement that the information may be subject to redisclosure.
- Do NOT use the word 'General Release' — HIPAA requires a specific-purpose authorization, not a general release.
- For minors' records, the signing party is the parent or legal guardian, and separate authorization MAY be required for certain adolescent services under Massachusetts law (e.g., M.G.L. c. 112, § 12F allows a minor to consent to emergency treatment, drug-dependency treatment, and reproductive health care without parental involvement).
- Psychotherapy notes under 45 C.F.R. § 164.501 require a SEPARATE, SPECIFIC authorization — a general HIPAA authorization does NOT cover them.
- Substance use disorder records under 42 C.F.R. Part 2 require explicit consent with the required notices; general HIPAA authorization is NOT sufficient.
- Target length: 2 pages.
hints: Massachusetts HIPAA Authorization — stand-alone instrument supplementing Health Care Proxy. Cite 45 C.F.R. § 164.508 for core elements: recipient identity, expiration, revocation, redisclosure notice. Sensitive categories (mental health per 42 CFR Part 2 / M.G.L. c. 123 § 36; HIV per M.G.L. c. 111 § 70F; substance use per 42 CFR Part 2) require express consent. Psychotherapy notes per 45 CFR § 164.501 require SEPARATE authorization. Post-death PHI protected 50 years per 45 CFR § 164.502(f). Massachusetts patient-rights law at M.G.L. c. 111, § 70E. Voluntary execution; Covered Entity may not condition treatment on signing (45 CFR § 164.508(b)(4)).
